name: Namur (Belgium)

colors.symbolism: borrowed from the national flag of Belgium
borrowed from the national flag of Belgium

description: A vertical bicolour of black at the hoist and red at the fly, in equal widths.
history: Adopted, together with the provincial arms, by the Provincial Council of Namur on 15 October 1953. The two colours are drawn from the black-yellow-red of the Belgian national flag.

features.symbolism: vertical bicolour with black at the hoist and red at the fly, the colours borrowed from the Belgian national flag
